summary: Sprint 1 addressed the most critical security gaps: SQL guard (SELECT-only with sqlparse), CORS restriction, error sanitization, and input validation. The remaining security surface is: (1) no SQL query execution timeout -- expensive SELECT can DoS the database, (2) no rate limiting on /api/query, (3) no read-only DB user, (4) Docker containers run as root, (5) no security headers on frontend, (6) LLM prompt injection not mitigated beyond SQL guard.

findings.title: No SQL query execution timeout -- DoS via expensive SELECT
findings.detail: execute_sql() runs text(sql) without any timeout. A crafted SELECT with cartesian joins could lock the DB connection indefinitely. The SQL guard only blocks non-SELECT, not expensive SELECTs.
findings.recommendation: Add SET LOCAL statement_timeout = 5000 before the query, or configure statement_timeout at the connection pool level.

findings.title: No rate limiting on /api/query endpoint
findings.detail: No rate limiting. Once LLM is wired, each request costs API tokens. Already in todo.md but critical before LLM goes live.

findings.title: No read-only database user for query execution
findings.detail: App connects with same credentials used for create_all(). Defense-in-depth requires a separate read-only connection for LLM query execution. Already in todo.md but still not addressed.
findings.recommendation: Create statbat_reader role with SELECT-only. Add READER_DATABASE_URL to settings. Use separate engine for execute_sql().

findings.title: Docker containers run as root user
findings.detail: No USER directive in Dockerfiles. Already tracked (SEC-009) but not implemented.
findings.recommendation: Add RUN adduser and USER directives to both Dockerfiles.

findings.title: No security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options) on frontend
findings.detail: No Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options headers. Clickjacking and XSS risks.
findings.recommendation: Add security headers in next.config.ts headers() function.

findings.title: API keys default to empty strings -- app starts silently without them
findings.detail: openai_api_key and anthropic_api_key default to ''. App starts without warning. Once LLM goes live, missing keys cause confusing runtime errors.
findings.recommendation: Log WARNING at startup if both API keys are empty.

findings.title: LLM prompt injection not mitigated beyond SQL guard
findings.detail: No input sanitization on user question before LLM. User could inject instructions. SQL guard mitigates destructive queries but LLM could be tricked into exfiltrating data via valid SELECTs.
findings.recommendation: Add prompt armor: wrap user input in delimiters, add explicit guardrails in system prompt, consider output validation.
